name: framework-migration-legacy-modernize
description: Orchestrate a comprehensive legacy system modernization using the strangler fig pattern, enabling gradual replacement of outdated components while maintaining continuous business operations through ex
capabilities: Analyze legacy codebase and generate modernization readiness report
Create dependency mapping and integration point catalog
Implement strangler fig infrastructure with API gateway routing
Orchestrate agent-based component modernization with test coverage
Execute progressive rollout with automated rollback triggers
Generate documentation and complete decommissioning plans
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged potential issues but manual review confirms all findings are false positives. Line 29 contains markdown file reference syntax (not shell backtick execution). Lines 3, 65, and 112 contain prompt template text with no cryptographic algorithms. The skill is a legitimate legacy modernization workflow guide.
valueStatement: Transform outdated legacy systems into modern architectures without downtime using the strangler fig pattern. This skill orchestrates phased migrations with agent coordination for assessment, testing, and implementation.
limitations: Requires existing codebase to analyze - cannot create new systems from scratch
Does not execute actual code changes - provides guidance and prompts only
Migration speed depends on team capacity and system complexity
May need manual intervention for highly coupled legacy components

useCases.title: Enterprise Application Migration
useCases.description: Migrate large monolithic applications to microservices while maintaining business continuity with phased rollout.

useCases.title: Ruby on Rails Legacy Upgrade
useCases.description: Modernize older Rails applications by gradually extracting components to modern frameworks.

useCases.title: Legacy API Modernization
useCases.description: Replace deprecated REST APIs with modern implementations while supporting existing consumers through facade patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Legacy Assessment Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze our legacy system at [ codebase_path ] for modernization readiness. Create a technical debt inventory including outdated dependencies, deprecated APIs, and security vulnerabilities. Generate a modernization readiness report with component complexity scores.
promptTemplates.scenario: Initial assessment phase to understand scope and priorities

promptTemplates.title: Migration Roadmap Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Based on our legacy assessment report, create a prioritized migration roadmap using the strangler fig pattern. Include dependency mapping, integration points requiring facade patterns, and risk mitigation strategies for each component.
promptTemplates.scenario: Planning phase to define migration sequence

promptTemplates.title: Component Modernization
promptTemplates.prompt: Modernize the following components from our prioritized roadmap: [ component_list ]. Extract business logic, implement using modern patterns with dependency injection and SOLID principles, ensure backward compatibility through adapter patterns.
promptTemplates.scenario: Execution phase for actual code modernization

promptTemplates.title: Progressive Rollout Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement progressive rollout strategy using feature flags. Start with 5% traffic to modernized components. Define automatic rollback triggers: error rate >1%, latency >2x baseline. Create rollout plan: 5% → 25% → 50% → 100% with 24-hour observation periods.
promptTemplates.scenario: Production rollout with safe rollout procedures

bestPractices: Establish comprehensive test coverage (>80%) before migrating each component to ensure behavior preservation
Use feature flags for all traffic routing to enable instant rollback if issues arise
Maintain backward compatibility through adapter patterns until full migration is validated
antiPatterns: Big-bang migration without parallel system operation - increases risk of complete system failure
Migrating critical revenue components first without adequate testing infrastructure
Skipping legacy assessment and jumping directly to code modernization

faq.question: What is the strangler fig pattern?
faq.answer: The strangler fig pattern is a migration approach where new functionality gradually replaces legacy components. Like a fig tree that envelops its host, the new system grows around the old one until it fully replaces it. This allows both systems to coexist during transition.

faq.question: How long does a typical legacy migration take?
faq.answer: Migration timeline depends on system complexity. Small applications may take 3-6 months, while enterprise systems with hundreds of components can take 12-24 months. The skill provides phased guidance to accelerate this process.

faq.question: Can this skill work with any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes, the strangler fig pattern is language-agnostic. The skill provides prompts that can target different agent types for Python, Go, Ruby, or other languages based on your target stack.

faq.question: What happens if migration causes issues in production?
faq.answer: The skill implements automatic rollback triggers based on error rates and latency. Feature flags allow instant traffic shifting back to the legacy system if problems are detected.

faq.question: Do we need to rewrite all the legacy code?
faq.answer: No, the strangler fig pattern allows gradual replacement. You can migrate component by component while the legacy system continues operating. Some legacy components may remain indefinitely if they are stable and not worth migrating.

faq.question: How do we ensure data consistency during migration?
faq.answer: The skill includes contract testing and dual-write strategies to maintain data consistency between legacy and modernized components. Event sourcing patterns can be implemented for complex data synchronization needs.
